items.headline: Tech Rally Counters Geopolitical Jitters as Futures Point Higher
items.summary: U.S. stock futures are showing upward movement this Wednesday, extending gains after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite closed at fresh record highs on Tuesday. Investor sentiment is largely driven by optimism surrounding artificial intelligence (AI) and chipmakers, despite ongoing geopolitical uncertainties in the Middle East. Lower oil prices are also contributing to the positive sentiment.

items.statContext: Up 0.31% today; Closed at 7,519.12 (+0.6%) on Tuesday.
Closed at 26,656.18 (+1.2%) on Tuesday, a record high. Nasdaq 100 closed at 30,001 (+1.76%). Futures up 0.38% today.
Slipped 0.2% on Tuesday, closing at 50,461.68. Futures up 0.46% today.

items.headline: Digital Turbine Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Shares of Digital Turbine surged after the company reported a strong FQ4 earnings beat and issued FY2027 guidance above expectations.

items.headline: Dycom Industries Rallies on Revenue Beat
items.summary: Dycom Industries saw its shares rise significantly after reporting first-quarter contract revenue that exceeded average analyst estimates.

items.headline: Semtech Gains on Positive Q1 and Q2 Outlook
items.summary: Semtech shares rose after the company's Q1 results topped expectations, and it provided stronger-than-expected Q2 guidance.

items.headline: Zscaler Drops on Weak Revenue Guidance
items.summary: Zscaler experienced a significant drop in after-hours trading on Tuesday following the release of weak revenue guidance.

items.headline: PDD Holdings Declines After Q1 Revenue Miss
items.summary: PDD ADRs fell after the company reported first-quarter revenue that missed the average analyst estimate.

items.headline: Insulet Falls on Medical Device Correction
items.summary: Insulet shares declined in after-hours trading on Tuesday after announcing a medical device correction.

items.headline: Technology and Chipmakers Continue to Lead Market Gains
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly chipmakers, remains the strongest force lifting the market, fueled by sustained optimism surrounding artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure and advanced computing systems. Energy, conversely, has been observed lagging.

items.headline: U.S. Consumer Confidence Remains Stable in May
items.summary: U.S. Consumer Confidence for May came in at 93.1, slightly above the consensus estimate of 92.9, indicating stable consumer sentiment. Additionally, Case-Shiller home prices for 10-City Composites increased 1.2% in March.

items.headline: ECB Warns Markets Underestimate Geopolitical and Fiscal Risks
items.summary: The European Central Bank (ECB) issued a warning that investors are underestimating the risks posed by the Middle East conflict and rising government debts. The ECB noted that financial asset prices appear 'stretched by historical standards.'

items.headline: Key Companies Report Earnings Today
items.summary: Several major companies are scheduled to report earnings today, May 27, 2026, including PDD Holdings ($PDD), Salesforce ($CRM), Snowflake ($SNOW), HP, Inc. ($HPQ), Synopsys ($SNPS), and Marvell ($MRVL). Investors will be closely watching for financial updates and forward guidance.

items.headline: BMO Financial Group Exceeds Q2 Expectations
items.summary: BMO Financial Group ($BMO) reported strong second-quarter 2026 results, with net income of $2,630 million and EPS of $3.53, both significantly higher than the prior year and exceeding expectations.

items.headline: U.S. and Mexico Announce USMCA Review Rounds
items.summary: The United States and Mexico have announced a series of bilateral negotiating rounds related to the first Joint Review of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA), with discussions on economic security and rules of origin for industrial goods starting this week.
